memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Shelton, Sheltone and others. First found in Yorkshire where they were seated from early times and their first records appeared on the census rolls taken by the ancient Kings of Britain to determine the rate of taxation of their subjects. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Richard and Francis Shelton who settled in Virginia in 1638; Samuel and Esther Shelton settled in Boston Mass. in 1765 with Hannah; Samuel Shelton settled in the Barbados with his servants in 1680. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This interesting surname, with variant spellings Shilton and Skelton is found widespread in the county of Nottinghamshire. The name itself is of English habitational origin from any of the places so called in Nottinghamshire, Bedfordshire, Norfolk, Shropshire and Staffordshire. The first of these was recorded "Sceltun", in the Domesday Book of 1086, while this source also records the other place names "Eseltone", "Sceltuna", "Saltone", and "Scelfitone", respectively. The place name is composed of the elements "scylf", the Old English word for shelf, ledge, or bank and "tun", the Old English word describing a settlement or enclosure. One John de Schelton appeared in 1260 in the Assize Court Rolls of Yorkshire, while the Assize Court Rolls of Warwickshire record a John Shelton in 1385. Notable name bearers include one Thomas Shelton (died 1620) who was the first translator of "Don Quixote" into English and one Sir Richard Shelton (died 1647) employed by the Duke of Buckingham and appointed solicitor-general (1625-1634) becoming M.P. for Bridgnorth (1626-1628) and treasurer of the Inner Temple in 1628. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Robert de Schelton, which was dated 1191, in the Pipe Rolls of Staffordshire, during the reign of King Richard 1st, known as "The Lionheart", 1189 - 1199. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Agatha Shelton於1308年出生在英國德比夏爾蒙亞申時，她的父親Sir John De Shelton, III59歲，母親Alice de Burgullion47歲。她和John I Jernegan至少有1個兒子。她去世在英國素福爾可索莫利頓。

zh-hans.Shelton, Norfolk, England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可雪登
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agatha Shelton于1308年出生在英国德比夏爾蒙亞申时，她的父亲Sir John De Shelton, III59岁，母亲Alice de Burgullion47岁。她和John I Jernegan至少有1个儿子。她去世在英国素福爾可索莫利頓。

nameMeanings.name: Shelton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): habitational name primarily from Shelton (Nottinghamshire), but also from Shelton (Bedfordshire, Norfolk, Shropshire, and Staffordshire). The placenames all derive from Old English scelf ‘rock, ledge, shelf’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Agatha Shelton was born in 1308, in Monyash, Derbyshire, England, her father, Sir John De Shelton, III, was 59 and her mother, Alice de Burgullion, was 47. She had at least 1 son with John I Jernegan. She died in Somerleyton, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agatha Shelton est née en 1308, à Monyash, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John De Shelton, III, avait 59 ans et sa mère, Alice de Burgullion, avait 47 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec John I Jernegan. Elle est décédée à Somerleyton, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agatha Shelton 1308 in Monyash, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John De Shelton, III 59 und ihre Mutter Alice de Burgullion 47. Sie hatte mit John I Jernegan mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in Somerleyton, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agatha Shelton è nata il nel 1308 nel Monyash, Derbyshire, England, sua padre, Sir John De Shelton, III, aveva 59 anni e sua madre, Alice de Burgullion, aveva 47 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con John I Jernegan. Morì nel Somerleyton, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agatha Sheltonが1308年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアモニーアッシュで生まれた時，父Sir John De Shelton, IIIは59歳，母Alice de Burgullionは47歳であった。彼女はJohn I Jerneganとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女はイギリススフォルクソメルリートンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agatha Shelton(이)가 1308년 영국 더비실르 몬야시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John De Shelton, III(은)는 59세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Burgullion(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 John I Jernegan(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 수폴크 서머리턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agatha Shelton nasceu em 1308, em Monyash, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John De Shelton, III, tinha 59 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Burgullion, tinha 47 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com John I Jernegan. Ela faleceu em Somerleyton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agatha Shelton родилась в 1308 г. в местности моняш, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John De Shelton, III было 59 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Burgullion, 47. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от John I Jernegan. Она умерла в местности сомирлитон, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agatha Shelton nació en 1308, en Monyash, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John De Shelton, III, tenía 59 años y su mamá, Alice de Burgullion, tenía 47 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con John I Jernegan. Murió en Somerleyton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
